project: kcl/tarray — port of FPC rtl-generics TArrayHelper (Sort/BinarySearch/scan helpers) to bash (kklass static class over caller-named arrays)
status: COMPLETE (P0-P4) 2026-07-13, awaiting owner review, UNCOMMITTED (commits gated). Full TArrayHelper port over caller-named arrays: stable bottom-up mergesort (byte/-n/cmpFn modes, range form), FPC-verbatim binarySearch (RESULT/CANDIDATE/COMPARE), indexOf family, min/max, strict copy, temp-safe reverse, concat, compact. FPC fpcunit seed mined as the oracle (006). Suite 001-007 = 80/80 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9; final master sweep 16/16 (see P4 log). Deliverables: tarray.sh, tests/001-007+tests.sh, README.md, docs/TArray.md, bench.sh, TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md, PLAN.md, ledger. Roadmap 3/7 done; next unit tinifile; APPROVED side-task queued: TList/TStringList delegation to TArray (owner 2026-07-13). Phases: P0 (S1-S8 pins + LC_ALL=C + skeleton), P1 (sort 28), P2 (search 62), P3 (copy/reverse/concat 80), P4 (docs/bench).
notes: Single source of truth for tarray status. Plan: kcl/tarray/PLAN.md. Source: generics.collections.pas TCustomArrayHelper :73-102 (+TBinarySearchResult :68-71), TArrayHelper :106-138; impl introsort ~:990-1220, BinarySearch :1225-1310/:1457-1520. Positioning: NOT a speed contest with /usr/bin/sort — the wins are zero forks + lossless arbitrary-string elements (newline-safe, where line-based sort corrupts) + binary search; niche n <= ~5k. Workflow: phase -> dual-bash -> master sweep -> STOP -> 'go'; commits gated.

baseline.measured: 2026-07-12 — at roadmap planning time (post-tdictionary). RE-MEASURE at P0: roadmap units 1-2 will have raised the totals.

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130

baseline.regression_rule: tarray work must keep the (re-measured) baseline green; the new tarray suite adds on top.

design_decisions.by_name_dense: Static methods over caller INDEXED array names via declare -n (__ta_ local prefix; __ta_* caller names rejected). Density contract 0..n-1 (FPC data model); sparse input NOT silently compacted — explicit TArray.compact; density check only under VERBOSE_KKLASS=debug. Assoc arrays rejected via ${arr@a} (rc=1).
design_decisions.default_comparators: String mode = BYTE order under scoped LC_ALL=C (deterministic across machines; locale collation is a documented non-goal). Numeric mode (-n) = 64-bit integer via (( )); non-integer element -> rc=1, NO silent lexicographic fallback. 10# guards against octal.
design_decisions.custom_comparator: Plain bash function (NOT a kklass method — 0.5 ms dispatch x n log n would be catastrophic). Protocol: cmpFn a b -> return code 0 (a<b) / 1 (equal) / 2 (a>b); no stdout (echo would force a $() fork per comparison). Built-in modes are INLINED in the merge loop.
design_decisions.algorithm: Bottom-up iterative MERGESORT: no recursion, O(n log n) worst case (matches introsort's goal), STABLE. Parity: FPC introsort's equal-element order is UNSPECIFIED, so stability is a strictly stronger documented guarantee (bash-side improvement row). O(n) scratch is fine at target sizes.
design_decisions.binary_search_shape: One call yields both FPC overload shapes: rc 0/1 + RESULT=FoundIndex(-1 miss) + RESULT_CANDIDATE + RESULT_COMPARE (TBinarySearchResult :68-71 flattened to RESULT_* convention). Empty array -> (-1,-1,0,rc1) verbatim :1231-1237. Sorted-under-same-comparator precondition (GIGO like FPC, documented).

goals: Faithful TArrayHelper surface: sort (3 forms), binarySearch (both result shapes), indexOf/firstIndexOf/lastIndexOf, contains, min/max (aDefault), copy (2 forms), reverse (copy-into-target), concat; extras reverseInPlace/compact documented as bash-convention.
Zero forks everywhere incl. the comparator hot path (rc-protocol); PATH='' tests.
Lossless arbitrary-string elements: '', newlines, globs, unicode — the correctness edge over line-based sort.
Determinism: byte-order default, no locale drift, stable sort.

test_basis.seed_primary: FPC SEED FOUND at P0 (2026-07-12): packages/rtl-generics/tests/tests.generics.arrayhelper.pas — a dedicated fpcunit test for TArrayHelper (BinarySearch/IndexOf/First/Last/Min/Max/Contains/Reverse), fixture a=(1 3 5 7 9 11 13 15 20). It is the parity ORACLE, mined into tests/00X_FpcParity.sh. NB: it has NO Sort test -> sort parity = the sorted-invariant + hand matrices. Also present: tqsort_killer.pp (sort stress), tests.generics.bugs.pas. Secondary basis: impl-source reading (generics.collections.pas line refs in PLAN) for S2/S4/S6/S7 sign+bounds detail.

parity_model.tier_A_exact: Sorted output, search indices/candidates, scan indices, min/max picks, copy/reverse/concat contents — byte-for-byte vs source-pinned semantics and oracles.

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: Non-FPC cases (torture elements, stability proof, sparse/assoc rejects, zero-fork) -> TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md rows.

out_of_scope.reason: Comparator is a plain bash function with an rc protocol; object protocol is dispatch overhead with no gain.
The algorithm is not the contract; the sorted result is. Bottom-up mergesort delivers the same O(n log n) bound plus stability.
Elements are bash strings; -n covers int64; float ordering routes through kcl/math in user comparators (README example), not here.

execution_log.summary: tarray PLAN.md + ledger written; folders created. No implementation. Key decisions recorded: bottom-up stable mergesort (vs FPC introsort — equal-order unspecified there, so stability is parity-compatible and stronger), comparator = plain function with rc 0/1/2 protocol (fork-free, dispatch-free), byte-order default under scoped LC_ALL=C, dense-array contract with explicit compact, TBinarySearchResult flattened to RESULT_* in one call.

execution_log.batch: P0 — semantics pin + design freeze + skeleton
execution_log.summary: Owner 'go tarray'. P0.1 + P0.2 done. FPC SEED FOUND (verified, not assumed — tregex lesson): tests.generics.arrayhelper.pas is the parity oracle. Read generics.collections.pas impl for the sign/bounds detail. LC_ALL=C idiom probed on BOTH bashes. Skeleton + runner + 001 smoke = 6/6 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.

pins.S1_binsearch_empty: found=false, FoundIndex=-1, CandidateIndex=-1, CompareResult=0 (impl :1231-1237; test Test_BinarySearch_EmptyArray).
pins.S2_binsearch_loop: Compare(AValues[mid], AItem) — ARRAY element FIRST. <0 go right, >=0 go left, =0 found. CandidateIndex = convergence imin; CompareResult = sign(array[candidate]-item); miss candidate = insertion point. Test fixture (1 3 5 7 9 11 13 15 20): search 10 -> candidate 5, found -1, compare>0 (array[5]=11>10); search 20 -> candidate 8, found 8, compare 0 (impl :1243-1292). cmpFn rc->sign map: {0:-1, 1:0, 2:+1}.
pins.S3_indexof: IndexOf == FirstIndexOf (first occurrence); LastIndexOf = last. impl :1371 (IndexOf delegates to FirstIndexOf); test: 9 in (1 3 5 7 9 11 13 9 20) -> first 4, last 7. Contains == IndexOf<>-1 (impl :1342).
pins.S4_reverse: builds into a TEMP buffer then assigns target, so src==dst (in-place) is SAFE; Reverse(a,b) leaves a untouched (impl :1324-1339; test does both Reverse(a,b) and Reverse(a,a)).
pins.S5_minmax: return the min/max VALUE (not index) or aDefault when empty; values so no tie ambiguity (test: 1/20; [] -> -1 default).
pins.S6_copy: same-array -> ERROR (SErrSameArrays); count<0 or > len(src)-srcIdx or > len(dst)-dstIdx -> ErrorArgumentOutOfRange; dst NOT auto-grown (impl :1301-1322). Copy(src,dst,count)=Copy(src,dst,0,0,count).
pins.S7_concat: TotalLen = sum of arg lengths; empty args skipped (CurLen>0 guard); all-empty -> empty (impl :1347-1369).
pins.S8_sort_range: range [AIndex .. AIndex+ACount-1] inclusive; ACount<=1 -> no-op (Exit); outside untouched (impl :1059-1065).

design_freeze.lc_idiom: byte order via `local LC_ALL=C` (NOT LC_COLLATE). Probed on 5.2.37 AND 5.3.9: `local LC_ALL=C` forces strcmp `[[ < ]]` (a<B FALSE, Z<a TRUE) regardless of the ambient locale, incl. ambient LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8; `local LC_COLLATE=C` FAILS under ambient LC_ALL (LC_ALL wins). Var-assignment prefix `LC_ALL=C [[ ... ]]` is NOT usable ([[ is a keyword). So: `local LC_ALL=C` at the top of the compare/sort/search functions.
design_freeze.comparator: cmpFn a b -> rc 0=a<b / 1=a==b / 2=a>b, fork-free (no $()); built-in string (LC_ALL=C [[ < ]]) and numeric (-n, (( )) 64-bit, shape ^-?[0-9]+$, 10# guard) modes INLINED.
design_freeze.algorithm: bottom-up iterative STABLE mergesort; O(n) scratch local.
design_freeze.contract: all members static proc (silent, call direct); RESULT / RESULT_CANDIDATE / RESULT_COMPARE; arrays by name (declare -n, __ta_ prefix, reject __ta_* caller names); dense indices; assoc arrays rejected rc=1.

execution_log.skeleton: kcl/tarray/tarray.sh (guard + kklass_pascal + class TArray with 13 static-proc members + P0 stubs) + tests/tests.sh runner + tests/001_Skeleton.sh (source+build, 13 dispatch, direct-sets-RESULT+silent, $()-no-leak, RESULT_CANDIDATE/COMPARE shaped, PATH='' zero-fork). 6/6 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.
execution_log.baseline_remeasured: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 16/16 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.', 2717 tests (was 15 suites/2711 pre-tarray; +6 from the tarray skeleton). tarray suite 6/6. No regression: only ADDS a suite; no existing unit or kklass file touched.

execution_log.summary: Owner 'go P1'. TArray.sort implemented as bottom-up iterative STABLE mergesort. Suite 6 -> 28 (002 modes/range/edges/rejects, 003 stability/torture/zero-fork). 28/28 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9. Sort has NO FPC seed test, so basis = the sorted-invariant + hand matrices + the stability definition.

implemented.signature: sort arr [cmp|-n] [start count]. arg after arr: '-n' -> numeric; a `declare -F` function name -> custom cmpFn; else it's `start` (default byte mode). start/count = the range form.
implemented.modes: default = BYTE order via `local LC_ALL=C` set ONLY in str mode (num uses (( )), fn may want the ambient locale). numeric -n = 64-bit (( )) over a PARALLEL normalized-int key array (10# strips leading-zero octal trap; bare (( 08 )) is fatal), ORIGINAL element strings preserved (007 stays 007). cmpFn = rc 0=a<b/1=a==b/2=a>b, INLINED per §2.4 (case branch, no per-compare function call for the 2 built-ins; fn called directly).
implemented.algorithm: bottom-up iterative mergesort, O(n) local scratch (__ta_buf/__ta_nbuf); STABLE (tie -> take LEFT run). Strictly stronger than FPC introsort (equal-order unspecified there) -> a documented bash-side guarantee.
implemented.range: S8: [start, start+count-1]; count<=1 no-op; start clamped >=0, start>=n no-op, start+count clamped to n.
implemented.rejects: associative array (${arr@a} has 'A') -> rc 1; non-integer element under -n -> rc 1 (array unchanged, no silent lexicographic fallback); missing array name -> rc 2.

execution_log.tests: 002 (byte-order incl. digit<upper<lower, numeric negatives/zeros/leading-zeros/64-bit, cmpFn bylen/revnum, range + clamp + count<=1, empty/single/all-equal/sorted/reversed, reject assoc/non-int/no-name); 003 (stability via tagged value:seq + byfirst, torture: newline/glob/space/empty/quotes/backticks/$()-looking/unicode/sentinel-as-data elements lossless byte-order, 200-element non-decreasing correctness, PATH='' zero-fork all 3 modes).
execution_log.test_fix: 001 updated for sort-now-real: the 'all 13 dispatch rc0' + zero-fork-on-stub-sort assertions were sort-as-stub; changed to '12 pending members return sentinel' (sort excluded) and zero-fork now sorts a real array (3 1 2 -> 1 2 3).
execution_log.sweep_gate: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 16/16 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.', 2739 tests (was 2717 at P0; +22 from tarray 6->28). No regression: no existing unit or kklass file touched.

execution_log.summary: Owner 'go'. binarySearch + indexOf/firstIndexOf/lastIndexOf/contains + min/max implemented via shared _resolveCmp (-n/fn/byte) + _cmp3 (3-way sign, dynamic scope). Suite 28 -> 62 (004 binarySearch, 005 scan family, 006 FPC parity). 62/62 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9. FPC ORACLE (006) confirms the port against tests.generics.arrayhelper.pas — 0-based, NO index adjustment (unlike tregex's 1-based Match.Index).

implemented._cmp3: 3-way compare -> __ta_sign -1/0/+1. str: LC_ALL=C [[ < ]]/[[ == ]] (caller sets local LC_ALL=C). num: 10#-guarded normalize (malformed -> 0, crash-free, documented precondition violation). fn: __ta_cmp rc {0:-1,1:0,2:+1}.
implemented.binarySearch: FPC loop verbatim (:1243-1292): Compare(array[mid],item) ARRAY-first; RESULT=FoundIndex(-1 miss), RESULT_CANDIDATE=convergence imin, RESULT_COMPARE=last sign; empty/empty-range -> -1/-1/0 rc1. Optional [start count] range. Cross-checked: miss(10)->cand5/found-1/cmp>0, hit(20)->cand8/found8/cmp0, empty->-1/-1/0.
implemented.scan: firstIndexOf (linear, first match), indexOf==firstIndexOf (S3), lastIndexOf (reverse linear), contains==firstIndexOf-rc, min/max (value or aDefault on empty, rc 0 found / 1 empty, tie->first). All accept [cmp]; min/max also [default].
implemented.modes_all: every member: default byte (local LC_ALL=C), -n numeric, or a cmpFn name; original element strings preserved.

execution_log.tests: 004 (hit/miss-below/miss-above candidate+compare-sign, empty/single, byte/cmpFn modes, range form, sort+search closure, newline torture, no-name rc2); 005 (indexOf==first/last on dups, contains present/absent/empty, min/max value+default+preserve-strings+single+tie-first, byte/cmpFn modes, newline/unicode torture, PATH='' zero-fork); 006 FPC-traceable (Test_BinarySearch_Integers/EmptyArray, IndexOf/First/Last, Min/Max, Contains — verbatim fixtures + expected values).
execution_log.test_fix: 001 updated for P2-now-real: pending-stub list trimmed to the 5 P3 members (copy/reverse/reverseInPlace/concat/compact); the direct-RESULT contract test switched from now-real min to the copy stub. 005 rc-capture order fixed (r=$? BEFORE d=$RESULT — an assignment resets $?).
execution_log.sweep_gate: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 16/16 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.', 2773 tests (was 2739 at P1; +34 from tarray 28->62). No regression: no existing unit or kklass file touched.

execution_log.summary: Owner 'go'. Remaining helpers implemented; all 13 members now real. Suite 62 -> 80 (007 + 006 Test_Reverse). 80/80 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.

implemented.reverse: reverse src dst -> dst=reversed src via a TEMP buffer, so src==dst (in-place) is SAFE (S4, impl :1324-1339); src untouched when distinct. rc 0/2.
implemented.copy: copy src dst [srcIdx dstIdx] count (3 or 5 args). S6 (impl :1301-1322): same NAME -> rc 2 (SErrSameArrays); count<0 or > len(src)-srcIdx or > len(dst)-dstIdx -> rc 2; dst NOT auto-grown (STRICT — the P0-flagged decision resolved FPC-faithful; a caller pre-sizes dst). count 0 -> no-op rc 0.
implemented.concat: concat dst src1 [src2 ...] (array NAMES). Empty sources skipped (S7, impl :1347-1369); built in a temp so dst may be a source. rc 0/2.
implemented.compact: bash extra: arr=("${arr[@]}") re-indexes sparse->dense, order kept (the ONE place holes drop, §2.1).
implemented.note: these are pure mutators — they set NO RESULT; call directly ($() discards array writes). Reserved names: __ta_src/__ta_dst/__ta_tmp/__ta_cur/__ta_arr.

execution_log.tests: 007 (reverse into-target + in-place + empty/single + rc2; copy count/indexed forms + same-array rc2 + dst-too-small-not-grown rc2 + count>src/negative rc2 + count-0 no-op; concat multi + skip-empty + dst-is-src + all-empty + no-name rc2; compact sparse->dense; torture reverse/concat lossless; PATH='' zero-fork); 006 extended with FPC Test_Reverse (into-target + in-place, source-safe).
execution_log.design_note: S6 copy resolved STRICT (dst not auto-grown) per the P0 flag — FPC-faithful, avoids silently creating sparse/partial arrays; documented in README/docs.
execution_log.test_fix: 001 updated for all-real: 'pending stubs' replaced by 'representative members real (sort/min/reverse behave)'; direct-RESULT contract test switched from the copy stub to real min (a value-returner).
execution_log.sweep_gate: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 16/16 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.', 2791 tests (was 2773 at P2; +18 from tarray 62->80). No regression: no existing unit or kklass file touched.

execution_log.summary: Owner 'go P4' (after a consultative detour: TArray-vs-TList difference + can-TArray-be-a-base-class -> NO inheritance (kklass static has no instance semantics; FPC itself composes: generics.collections :2151-2168 TList<T>.Sort = one-line TArrayHelper.Sort(FItems,cmp,0,Count)); owner approved a SEPARATE follow-up task: TList/TStringList delegation to TArray). Unit closed out: README.md (contract, API, comparator worked example, measured positioning vs /usr/bin/sort, FPC-parity note), docs/TArray.md (upstream per-member reference + wontfix reasons), bench.sh (dog-foods TStopwatch.getTimeStamp per the house bench-clock convention). TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES finalized.
execution_log.bench: bash bench.sh on MSYS2 both bashes (5.2.37 / 5.3.9): sort n=100 ~220/175 us/elem (22/17 ms total), n=1000 ~283/280 us/elem, n=5000 ~373/370 us/elem (1.9 s); mode delta at n=1000: byte 294/288, -n 372/346, cmpFn 395/371 us/elem; binarySearch on n=5000 ~0.83/1.33 ms/search vs indexOf full-scan miss ~288/479 ms (~350x); reverse ~7-11 us/elem, concat ~2-3 us/elem; INFORMATIONAL one printf|sort fork+pipe n=1000 ~45/57 ms. POSITIONING: below ~300-500 elements TArray.sort BEATS forking sort (22 vs 45 ms at n=100); at thousands the external tool wins raw speed (50 ms vs 1.9 s at n=5000) IF elements are newline-free; TArray's wins = zero forks, lossless arbitrary strings, stability+cmpFn, binarySearch, FPC API. zero-fork check GREEN on all 13 members, both bashes.
execution_log.bench_note: bench.sh is NOT run by the master sweep (tests/tests.sh discovers only tests/tests.sh); validated by running on both bashes -- numbers sane + zero-fork green.
execution_log.status_final: tarray COMPLETE (P0-P4). Suite 001-007 = 80/80 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9. Deliverables all present. UNCOMMITTED (commits gated). FINAL MASTER SWEEP (5.2.37, absolute path): 16/16 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.', 2791 tests -- clean.
execution_log.followup_approved: TList/TStringList -> TArray delegation (owner 'Да' 2026-07-13): wire TList.Sort/CustomSort/Find stubs + TStringList.Sort bubble to TArray.sort/binarySearch '${__inst__}_items' cmp 0 $count (FPC composition pattern); needs the RESULT-vs-rc comparator shim + case_sensitive-via-cmpFn (NOT byte default); own task + sweep gate (touches tlist 92 + tstringlist 155 tests).
